comments.author: Steve
comments.text: good enough working assumptions to start with

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Excellent point! I am pretty sure we can achieve this with the 7 tricks we have. You can cheat in two ways: iterate through a couple decks until you get lucky OR give yourself a bigger head start on the board.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Just to be clear, players hints DO change after every card gets played from the hand to the board. But for now, hints don't carry state, which is what I think you are saying.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: From the very beginning of the process, have the mindset that these scenarios are useful data to mine.  An ambiguous hand leads automatically to a nice test fixture or an easy judgment call for Steve to make.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Nope, keep it simple for now. I know exactly the nuance you are suggesting, but I am keeping it out of scope for v1.
